content: The human brain’s active learning memory mechanism, which consolidates weakly encoded episodic traces into long-term knowledge based on how often they reactivate during planning, can be reverse-engineered to fix the stagnating intrinsic reward problem in Intrinsic Curiosity Modules (ICMs). This reimagined ICM would prioritize and consolidate memory traces of unexplored, prediction-error-heavy states instead of discarding them after a single curiosity signal, allowing autonomous agents to sustain long-term exploratory learning in open-ended environments.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
trigger_topic: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
